rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

rules_primary: If Donovan Clingan records a Double Double in the Brooklyn at Portland professional basketball game originally scheduled for Mar 23, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. rules_primary: If Deni Avdija records a Triple Double in the Brooklyn at Portland professional basketball game originally scheduled for Mar 23, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a triple double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 3 or more of these statistics results in a triple double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

rules_primary: If Jalen Suggs records a Double Double in the Indiana at Orlando professional basketball game originally scheduled for Mar 23, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

rules_secondary: Statistics are considered final at the conclusion of the specified time period of the basketball game. Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

Statistics that count towards a double double include points, rebounds, assists, blocks, and steals. Any combination of 10+ in 2 or more of these statistics results in a double double. Corrections made after the game ends will not affect resolution. Statistics recorded during both regulation time and overtime count unless the time period specifies otherwise (e.g., "regulation only"). If the game is cancelled before starting, markets resolve to last fair price. If cancelled after starting and not resumed within 24 hours, markets resolve to last fair price except for thresholds already reached. If an entity (player or team) withdraws before the game, markets on that entity resolve to last fair price. If a player is injured, retires, or is disqualified during the game after playing at least one second, markets settle based on end-of-game statistics.

rules_primary: If the play by play or color commentator(s) says Double Double as part of Kentucky vs Iowa St. College Basketball Game, then the market resolves to Yes.
rules_secondary:
Video of the Kentucky vs Iowa St. College Basketball Game will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, internal & external (official) transcripts of the Kentucky vs Iowa St. College Basketball Game will be used.

Payout Criterion: 
The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. 
Commentary will count once tip off has occurred and end once the game has concluded. Should the game go into overtime, the commentary will continue to count until the game has ended. If the final play is in review, commentary will end following the referee decision.
Promotional content aired during the event will count, but advertisements will not. 
Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
Please see full rules for more details.

Event does not qualify:
If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.  

Postponement:
If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.
title: What will the announcers say during Kentucky vs Iowa St. College Basketball Game?
